betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Semiconductor Engineering: Advanced Processor§f

§aThe Advanced Processor is the brain of every QMD ring accelerator controller!§f
The §bAdvanced Processor§f (`qmd:semiconductor:5`) is the mid-tier QMD logic chip, used to craft every ring-accelerator-class controller. It is assembled in an §bNC Assembler§f.

§6Recipe (QMDRecipes.java:504)§f:
Per `NCRecipes.assembler.addRecipe(...)`:
- Output: §b1 × Advanced Processor§f (`qmd:semiconductor:5`)
- Input 1: §bBasic Processor§f (`qmd:semiconductor:4`, 1 unit)
- Input 2: §bRedstone§f (`dustRedstone`, 4 units)
- Input 3: §bHafnium Oxide Dust§f (`qmd:chemical_dust:5`, 1 unit)
- Input 4: §bP-Type Doped Silicon§f (`qmd:semiconductor:0`, 1 unit)
- Time: 1 RF/t baseline, Power: 1 RF/t

§6Component Supply Chain§f:
- §bBasic Processor§f → requires §bN-Type Doped Silicon§f + 4 Redstone + Gold + Silver (per QMDRecipes.java:503). N-Type is made in a §bFission Irradiator§f.
- §bP-Type Doped Silicon§f → must be produced by Boron-11 ion bombardment in a §bTarget Chamber§f (no assembler recipe).
- §bHafnium Oxide§f (`qmd:chemical_dust:5`) → byproduct of the §bOre Leacher§f processing Hafnium-bearing ore.

§6Direct Descendants (graph-confirmed)§f:
The Advanced Processor is consumed in these crafting recipes:
- §bSynchrotron Accelerator Controller§f (`qmd:ring_accelerator_controller`)
- §bBeam Diverter Controller§f (`qmd:beam_diverter_controller`)
- §bBeam Splitter Controller§f (`qmd:beam_splitter_controller`)
- §bLaser Assembly§f (`qmd:part:10`)
- §bEmpty Wire Chamber§f (`qmd:part:7`)
- §bSilicon Tracker Detector§f (`qmd:particle_chamber_detector:1`)
- §bElite Processor§f (`qmd:semiconductor:6`, via assembler)

§cWarning§f: You cannot skip the Basic Processor tier — the Advanced Processor recipe explicitly requires a §cBasic Processor§f as one of its four inputs. P-Type Doped Silicon has §cno assembler recipe§f; you must first build a working Target Chamber to manufacture it.

§eTip§f: Build your P-Type production line §bbefore§f you commit to scaling up Synchrotron controllers — one Advanced Processor consumes 1 P-Type wafer, and you can produce P-Type only as fast as your Boron-11 beam and Target Chamber allow.
